cover.headline_html:
How Sonoma Design + Build
wins more
of the right clients.
cover.subtitle: A personalized marketing strategy for Sonoma Design + Build, built on what's actually working in BC's custom home market right now.

snapshot.headline_html:
A quick look at
Sonoma Design + Build online.
snapshot.lead: I took a close look at Sonoma Design + Build's digital presence, from your website to social media to Google. Here's an honest snapshot of where things are and where the biggest opportunities sit.
paragraphs: The good news: valid ssl certificate. Also, single h1 tag. Anyone who finds Sonoma Design + Build is going to see quality work.
The challenge: no structured data (schema.org). Google Business Profile not detected. These are fixable gaps with outsized upside.

insight.title: The Bottom Line
insight.content_html: Sonoma Design + Build builds great homes but the online presence doesn't fully reflect that yet. The good news is the fixes are straightforward, the competition in your market is beatable, and the upside is significant. This plan shows you exactly how to close the gap.

opportunity.headline_html:
What the top builders
in your market are doing.
opportunity.lead: The BC market has some strong custom home builders. But when you look at who's actually showing up online, the field thins out fast.
paragraphs: I looked at the builders in your area across every channel: website quality, social media activity, Google presence, professional photography, and awards. Some are doing it well. Most aren't. And that's your opportunity.

The builders consistently landing the best projects aren't necessarily building better homes than you. They're just more visible where it counts. They show up in Google, they post consistently on social, and they have the professional imagery to back it up.

insight.title: What This Means
insight.content_html: Only a handful of builders in your market are firing on all cylinders. The rest have the same gaps you do. That means this isn't about catching up to an impossible standard. It's about being one of the first to get it right. And right now, that position is wide open.

website.headline_html:
Your website is your
24/7 sales team.
website.lead: Sonoma Design + Build's website at www.sonomadesignbuild.ca has 10 pages. Here's what's working and where the biggest SEO wins are.
paragraphs: Your site doesn't have structured data markup (JSON-LD), which means Google can't pull your business details into rich search results. Adding LocalBusiness schema is the single fastest technical win available.
Dedicated project pages are where SEO and client trust intersect. Each completed build should have its own page with professional photos, specs, and location tags. These are what rank in Google.
Your sitemap is set up, which helps Google index your pages.

checklist.detail: Helps Google understand your business. LocalBusiness + Organization schema at minimum.
Each completed project gets its own page with professional photos, specs, and client context.
None and surrounding areas. One page per service area.
Every page needs a unique, compelling meta description under 160 characters.
Project case studies, build process insights, and local market content.
Every image needs descriptive alt text. Good for SEO and accessibility.

insight.title: The Professional Photography Connection
insight.content_html: Every recommendation above needs one thing to work: strong visual content. Project pages need professional photography. Blog posts need real images from real builds. Location pages need hero shots from that area. The website strategy and the content strategy are the same strategy.

gbp.lead: Sonoma Design + Build doesn't appear to have a fully optimized Google Business Profile. This is the single highest-ROI action in this entire plan.
paragraphs: When someone searches 'custom home builder BC', Google shows the map pack first. That's three businesses. GBP is how you get there.
The fix isn't complicated. A fully optimized GBP with 20+ reviews, professional photos, and weekly posts would put you in the map pack within 90 days.

checklist.text: Claim and verify your GBP listing
checklist.detail: If you haven't already. Google will mail a postcard or call to verify.
Business hours, services, description, attributes. 100% completion helps ranking.
Completed projects, team, office, process shots. Updated monthly.
Project milestones, completed builds, awards, team updates. Takes 5 minutes.
Ask every happy client. Respond to every review within 24 hours.
All areas you serve and surrounding communities.

insight.title: Why This Matters for Builders
insight.content_html: Your ideal client isn't scrolling Instagram looking for a builder. They're Googling 'custom home builder near me.' GBP is how you intercept that search. And the builders in your market who've figured this out are getting those calls instead of you.

social.lead: Most builders in the BC market are either not posting on social media or posting inconsistently. This is a wide-open opportunity.
paragraphs: This isn't about becoming a social media company. It's about showing the work you're already doing in a way that attracts the clients you want.
The builders getting the most traction on social aren't hiring agencies or spending hours on content. They're doing one thing well: showing the work in progress.

pillars.title: The Build
pillars.description: Progress shots, framing, structural moments. The stuff that shows your team knows what they're doing. Phone photos work fine here.

pillars.title: The Finished Product
pillars.description: Professional photography of completed projects. These are the posts that get saved and shared. This is what attracts the right clients.

pillars.title: The Team
pillars.description: Crew on site, team lunches, jobsite culture. People hire people. Show the humans behind the builds.

pillars.title: The Process
pillars.description: Material selections, design decisions, problem-solving. Educational content positions you as the expert.

insight.title: The Content Math
insight.content_html: One professional photo shoot produces 20-25 images. That's 5 weeks of social content from a single day. Add in your phone shots from site visits and team moments, and you have a full content calendar without ever running out of material.

content_engine.lead: This is where everything connects. Professional project documentation isn't just about having nice photos on your website. It's the engine that powers your entire marketing system.
paragraphs: One professional photo shoot of a completed project produces 20-25 edited images. Here's where those images go and what they do for you:

channels.title: Website & SEO
entries: Dedicated project page with full photo gallery
Blog case study with build story and specs
Location page hero images for local SEO
Schema markup with project imagery

channels.title: Social Media
entries: 5+ weeks of Instagram and Facebook content
LinkedIn portfolio posts for B2B credibility
Before/after carousels and reel content
Story content showing craftsmanship details

channels.title: Google Business Profile
entries: Monthly photo updates (boosts ranking)
Project completion posts with imagery
Service area photos tied to locations
Team and process documentation

channels.title: Sales & Proposals
entries: Client presentation decks with real imagery
Printed materials and brochures
Award submission photography (Georgie, CHBA)
Publication-ready images (Western Living, Dwell)

insight.title: The Multiplier Effect
insight.content_html: Most builders think of photography as a one-time expense for the website. The builders winning the most work treat it as a content investment. One day of shooting creates months of marketing fuel across every platform. The ROI isn't in the photos themselves, it's in what those photos do across your entire marketing system.

reviews.lead: For a builder of Sonoma Design + Build's caliber, 20+ Google reviews should be the minimum. Here's how to build that systematically.
paragraphs: Custom home building is a high-trust, high-ticket decision. Potential clients aren't buying shoes online. They're investing hundreds of thousands in their family's home. Reviews are how they validate their gut feeling about you before they ever pick up the phone.
The goal isn't hundreds of reviews. It's 20-30 genuine reviews from real clients that tell specific stories about working with you.

checklist.text: Send a review request within 1 week of project handover
checklist.detail: Strike while the excitement is fresh. Include a direct link to your Google review page.

checklist.text: Make it easy with a direct Google review link
checklist.detail: Don't send them to your website. Send the Google review URL so it's one click.

checklist.text: Respond to every review within 24 hours
checklist.detail: Thank them specifically. Mention the project. Shows potential clients you care.

checklist.text: Ask subcontractors and design partners too
checklist.detail: Architects, designers, and trades you work with can leave Google reviews about the collaboration.

checklist.text: Feature best reviews on your website
checklist.detail: Pull the strongest Google reviews onto your homepage and project pages.

insight.title: The Compounding Effect
insight.content_html: Reviews compound. The first 10 get you into consideration. The next 10 get you ranked. After 20+, you're the obvious choice. And every review with a specific story does more than any ad could.

referrals.headline_html:
Turn happy clients into
your sales team.
referrals.lead: Most of your best projects probably came from referrals. The problem is that referrals happen randomly. A system makes them predictable.
paragraphs: In BC, word of mouth is everything. But right now it's accidental. Someone mentions you at a dinner party, maybe the friend follows up, maybe they don't.
The builders consistently landing the best projects have three things in common: they ask, they make it easy, and they follow up.

steps.title: The Ask
steps.description: At project handover, ask directly: 'We loved working on this project. If you know anyone building or renovating, we'd appreciate the introduction.' Simple, honest, no incentive needed.

steps.title: Make It Easy
steps.description: Give them something to share. A link to the project page on your website with professional photos. People share beautiful things without being asked twice.

steps.title: The Follow-Up
steps.description: Thank every referrer personally. A handwritten note or a framed print of their home goes further than any gift card. Stay in their world with a quick check-in every 6 months.

insight.title: The Photography Connection
insight.content_html: The single best referral tool is a stunning project page with professional photography. When a past client tells their friend 'you should call our builder,' the friend Googles you. If what they find looks as good as the recommendation, you get the call. If it doesn't, you lose it.

awards.lead: Awards aren't vanity. They're the third-party validation that separates Sonoma Design + Build from every other builder making the same claims.
paragraphs: 'Award-winning custom home builder' in your Google listing, on your website, and in your proposals changes the conversation. Most builders haven't submitted. That's the opportunity.
A single strong submission with professional photography could land your first award this year.

channels.title: Award Programs
entries: Georgie Awards (CHBA BC) — deadline typically March
CHBA National Awards for Housing Excellence
HAVAN Awards (if Lower Mainland)
Gold Nugget Awards — international recognition

channels.title: Publications
entries: Western Living — BC's premier shelter magazine
Mountain Life — strong for Sea-to-Sky builders
Dwell — design-focused, high credibility
Local newspaper home sections and features

insight.title: The Requirement
insight.content_html: Every award submission and publication pitch requires one thing: professional photography. Not phone shots. Not point-and-shoot. Publication-quality imagery that meets specific technical requirements. Without it, your best project can't even enter the conversation.

roadmap.headline_html:
Where to start.
What to do first.
roadmap.lead: You don't need to do everything at once. Here's the prioritized sequence based on what will move the needle fastest for Sonoma Design + Build.

entries: Claim and fully optimize your Google Business Profile
Add 15-20 of your best project photos to GBP
Ask your 5 most recent clients for Google reviews
Fix any critical website issues (broken links, missing pages, slow load)
Start posting 3x/week on Instagram (build progress + finished work + team)
Create your first project case study page on the website
Schedule your first professional photo shoot of a completed project
Set up your LinkedIn company page
Publish 2 blog posts (project case study + local market insight)
Launch your review request system (email template + direct link)
Post weekly GBP updates with project photos
Research award submission deadlines and requirements
Second professional photo shoot (different project or in-progress build)
Submit first award application with professional imagery
Activate referral system with past client outreach
Review analytics: what's working, what to double down on

rhythm.lead: After the first 90 days, marketing becomes a rhythm, not a project. Here's what a sustainable monthly cadence looks like.

cards.title: Content & Social
entries: 3-4 Instagram posts (mix of build, finished, team)
1 LinkedIn post (project update or insight)
1 GBP post with photos
Reply to all comments and DMs

cards.title: Website & SEO
entries: 1 blog post or project case study
Update project gallery with new photos
Check Google Search Console for opportunities
Update GBP with new project photos

cards.title: Relationships
entries: Send 2-3 review requests to recent clients
Reach out to 1 past client for referral ask
Connect with 2 architects or designers
Engage with local builders and trades online

cards.title: Big Moves
entries: 1 professional photo shoot (completed project)
Review award submission deadlines
Audit website analytics and adjust strategy
Plan next quarter's content calendar

cards.title: Content Production
entries: Batch edit and distribute shoot images
Create 3 months of social content from shoot
Update website project pages
Prep award submission materials

cards.title: Strategy Review
entries: Full marketing audit (what worked, what didn't)
Update website with best projects from the year
Plan award submissions for the year
Set content and growth targets for next 12 months

insight.title: The Real Secret
insight.content_html: The builders who win at marketing aren't spending more time on it. They're spending better time. One professional photo shoot per quarter produces enough content to fuel everything else for months. The system runs itself once the content exists.

images.alt: Tranquil Retreat — wood ceiling living room

case_study.lead: Balmoral Construction is a custom home builder in the Sea-to-Sky corridor. Here's what happened when they invested in professional documentation and a content-driven marketing strategy.

header_image.alt: Warbler Residence — built by Balmoral Construction, Whistler
header_image.title_html:
From invisible online to
the builder people find first.

paragraphs: When we started working together, Balmoral had completed over 50 custom homes across the Sea-to-Sky corridor but had zero online presence to show for it. No project pages, no professional photography, no awards, no content strategy.
We built a full 42-page website with dedicated project pages, location-specific landing pages, and a blog. Every page is backed by professional architectural photography.
We then launched a monthly content system: professional photo shoots, social media content, Google Business Profile optimization, and award submissions.

The result: Balmoral now shows up in Google for every key search term in their market. Their website generates qualified leads. Their Instagram showcases the work. And for the first time, their online presence matches the quality of what they actually build.

insight.title: The Takeaway
insight.content_html: Balmoral didn't need to change how they build homes. They needed to show how they build homes. The photography, the website, the content system, they're all the same investment. And it compounds every month.

paragraphs: Everything in this plan starts with one thing: professional visual content. The website, the social, the awards, the reviews, they all need imagery that matches the quality of what you build.

I run a full-service creative agency for builders across BC. Photography, website design, content strategy, social media, and brand development, all under one roof. Not a vendor you call once a year, but an embedded creative partner who keeps your marketing engine running.

details.label: Service Area
details.value: Sea-to-Sky, Sunshine Coast, Vancouver, Fraser Valley

closing.headline_html:
This plan works.
If it gets executed.
paragraphs: Owner, I put this together because Sonoma Design + Build builds the kind of homes that should be getting more attention than they are. The work is there. The marketing just needs to catch up.

Every section of this plan connects back to one thing: having professional visual content to fuel the machine. Without it, the website stays thin, the social stays inconsistent, the awards stay unsubmitted, and the best clients keep finding your competitors first.
I specialize in exactly this, architectural and construction photography built specifically for marketing. Not just pretty photos, but a content system that feeds every channel you need.
If you'd like to talk about getting the content engine started, I'm here. No pitch, just a conversation about what would make the biggest difference for Sonoma Design + Build right now.

steps.title: Start with GBP
steps.description: The highest-ROI action in this plan. Takes an afternoon, pays off for years.

steps.title: Shoot One Project
steps.description: Pick your best recent build. One professional shoot creates months of content across every channel.

steps.title: Build the Rhythm
steps.description: Once the content exists, the weekly posting and monthly updates take minutes, not hours.
